---
title: "CDADE Results Report"
author: "CDADE Team"
date: "2026-07-05"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

# Results

## Experiment summary

The current run completed the full DVC workflow and generated evaluation outputs from the injected SIVEP and Tycho experiments. Per-dataset metrics are stored under results/metrics/{dataset}/metrics.json and the per-method summaries under results/evaluation/{dataset}/.

## Metrics across datasets

```{python}
#| label: fig-metrics-heatmap
#| fig-cap: "AUC-PR per method and dataset. Darker = higher AUC-PR."
import pathlib, sys
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

sys.path.insert(0, str(pathlib.Path("..").resolve()))
from cdade.evaluation.stats_matrix import _build_auc_pr_matrix_from_dir

metrics_dir = pathlib.Path("../results/metrics")
mat, method_names, dataset_names = _build_auc_pr_matrix_from_dir(metrics_dir)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(max(6, len(method_names) * 0.9), max(2, len(dataset_names) * 0.7)))
im = ax.imshow(mat, vmin=0, vmax=1, cmap="YlGn", aspect="auto")
ax.set_xticks(range(len(method_names))); ax.set_xticklabels(method_names, rotation=40, ha="right")
ax.set_yticks(range(len(dataset_names))); ax.set_yticklabels(dataset_names)
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="AUC-PR")
for i in range(len(dataset_names)):
    for j in range(len(method_names)):
        ax.text(j, i, f"{mat[i, j]:.2f}", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=8,
                color="black" if mat[i, j] < 0.7 else "white")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Statistical comparison

```{python}
#| label: fig-cd-diagram
#| fig-cap: "Critical-difference diagram. Methods connected by a bar are not significantly different (Wilcoxon + Bonferroni α=0.05)."
import json, pathlib
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import rankdata

wilcoxon_path = pathlib.Path("../results/stats/wilcoxon.json")

if len(dataset_names) < 2:
    print("CD diagram requires ≥2 datasets. Only one dataset available.")
else:
    ranks = np.apply_along_axis(lambda r: rankdata(-r), 1, mat)
    mean_ranks = ranks.mean(axis=0)

    wilcoxon = json.loads(wilcoxon_path.read_text()) if wilcoxon_path.exists() else {}
    nonsig_pairs = [
        (k.split(" vs ")[0].strip(), k.split(" vs ")[1].strip())
        for k, v in wilcoxon.items()
        if not v.get("significant", True)
    ]

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(max(7, len(method_names)), 2.5))
    ax.set_xlim(0.8, len(method_names) + 0.2)
    ax.axhline(1, color="black", linewidth=1.5)
    for i, (name, rank) in enumerate(zip(method_names, mean_ranks)):
        ax.plot(rank, 1, "o", color="#2c7bb6", markersize=8)
        va = "bottom" if i % 2 == 0 else "top"
        ax.text(rank, 1, f"  {name}\n  ({rank:.2f})", ha="center", va=va, fontsize=8)
    for (m1, m2) in nonsig_pairs:
        if m1 in method_names and m2 in method_names:
            r1 = mean_ranks[method_names.index(m1)]
            r2 = mean_ranks[method_names.index(m2)]
            ax.plot([min(r1, r2), max(r1, r2)], [1.15, 1.15], "-", color="#d7191c", linewidth=3)
    ax.set_xlabel("Mean rank (1 = best)")
    ax.set_yticks([])
    ax.spines[["left", "top", "right"]].set_visible(False)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

The statistical stage now runs both SIVEP and Tycho, feeding a real `(2, n_methods)` AUC-PR matrix into the Friedman omnibus test (see `results/stats/friedman.json`). With only two datasets and six comparable methods, the current run is not significant (`stat=3.0`, `p=0.558`), so the protocol correctly stops before Wilcoxon, Diebold-Mariano, and Cliff's delta are treated as confirmatory — those outputs are still written (see the effect-size summary in the ablation report) but should be read as exploratory until more datasets are added to the active set.

## Interpretation

On this first two-dataset run, CDADE matched or exceeded the strongest baselines on both SIVEP and Tycho, but the Friedman test found no statistically significant difference across methods given only two datasets. The results support the implementation workflow and the report structure described in the README; a larger active dataset set will be needed before the Friedman → Wilcoxon → Diebold-Mariano → Cliff's delta protocol can produce a confirmatory ranking.
